body.title: Thalamus intent classification reads graph weights — fix vision/peer misclassification as code_task
body.result: Added relational-pronoun guard in _classify_intent (thalamus.py): when input has both peer signals ('you and i', 'as peers', 'we work', etc.) AND vision/process signals ('drive claude', 'our process', 'end state', etc.), return conversation before code_task/action_request patterns can fire. Root cause was CC_FIND_TICKETS misfire f5bff77c 2026-03-23. Also added _graph_weight_intent_hint() stub as architecture hook for future WordGraph-injected intent correction (follow-up L ticket). Tests: 11 cases in tests/test_thalamus.py. Commit 3134b77.
body.description: Stage 1 preparse (word graph + thalamus) classifies intent without querying graph weights. Vision/peer conversation ('you and I work as peers', 'drive Claude's process') scores as code_task because no relational/aspirational graph signal is consulted. Fix: add graph-weight query step inside thalamus classification pass. Two parts: (1) thalamus.py: hook to query graph weights as classification inputs; (2) graph: seed weights mapping vision/peer/end-state signals toward general/conversation, suppressing code_task for relational-pronoun-heavy inputs. Root cause found: CC_FIND_TICKETS misfire turn f5bff77c 2026-03-23. D201 conditions gate on CC_FIND_TICKETS is the defensive fix already applied — this is the upstream fix.
